## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-stats
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard: Launch the interactive web dashboard to visualize a codebase's knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-diff

- understand-anything:understand-explain

- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

description: "VietNexus hackathon app — spec source, frontend/backend layout, what's real vs mocked"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory

---

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- The design/behavior spec is a bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` — content is gzip+base64 inside a `__bundler/manifest` script tag; the readable app template/logic is in the `__bundler/template` JSON (extract before reading).
- `frontend/` is Vite + React 18 + GSAP, built 2026-07-18 by parallel Sonnet subagents against a shared contract spec. Dev server proxies `/api/*` → gateway at :3000.
- `backend/gateway` is Express + Sequelize/Postgres: `/auth/register|login|me` (username+password+role founder|investor, JWT), `/profiles` CRUD. No CORS (proxy relied upon). Roles: frontend 'startup' maps to backend 'founder'.
- Match datasets are static in `frontend/src/data/ecosystem.js` — no matching engine exists yet; that's the obvious next build step.
- Consent + Draft/Ready status have no backend columns; they live in localStorage (`vn.session`, `vn.profile.<username>`).

VietNexus "Innovation OS" (dqplus-hackathon-2026, July 2026): startup↔investor matching app for the Vietnamese ecosystem.

- Design/behavior spec: bundled prototype at `/mnt/d/Obsidian/outputs/hackathon/VietNexus Onboarding.html` (gzip+base64 in `__bundler/manifest`; readable template in `__bundler/template` JSON).
- Services (all Express): `backend/gateway` :3000 (auth + profiles, Sequelize, JWT), `backend/matching-engine` :3002 (pgvector cosine + attribute scoring over `extracted_profiles`), `backend/agent/extract` :3003 locally (**3001 in .env.example but Uptime Kuma occupies 3001 on this machine**) — builds `extracted_profiles` rows + embeddings via FPT-cloud OpenAI-compatible API.
- All three share Postgres DB `gateway` (user/pass `dealflow`/`dealflow`) in the sibling project's container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`. pgvector was apt-installed into that running container on 2026-07-18 — **lost if the container is recreated**; durable fix is switching that compose to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Frontend (Vite+React+GSAP) has NO mock data as of 2026-07-18: matches come from the matching engine via vite proxies (`/api/matches`→:3002, `/api/extract`→:3003, `/api`→gateway :3000). Demo-mode fake login removed; session now stores `user.id` (older localStorage sessions are invalidated). Flow: "I'm ready" → save profile → POST /extract/profile → GET matches (404 → auto-extract → retry).
- **Hard blocker**: `OPENAI_API_KEY` in `backend/agent/extract/.env` is empty — extraction 401s until a key is provided, so no matches can be generated.
- No backend exists for the customers/partners/talent match intents (tabs show honest "not connected" notice), outreach-draft generation is a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`), and consent/draft-ready status still live only in localStorage (`vn.profile.<username>`).

content.text:
Done — the frontend no longer contains any mock data; the matches flow now runs entirely on the real backend pipeline. Everything is verified working end-to-end through the running preview server at `:5173`, except one blocker only you can resolve (API key, below).

## What changed

**Frontend (mocks removed)**
- `src/data/ecosystem.js`: deleted the entire fake datasets (`CANDIDATES`, `PARTNERS`, `CUSTOMERS`, `TALENT`, `STARTUPS`, `WHY_NOW`) — only UI config (sectors, stages, tab definitions) remains.
- `src/App.jsx` + `src/lib/api.js`: the matches page now calls the matching engine — founders get `GET /matches/founders/:userId/investors`, investors get `GET /matches/investors/:userId/founders`. Clicking "I'm ready" saves the profile, then calls the extract agent (`POST /extract/profile`) to refresh the embedding, then loads matches. If matches 404 (no extracted profile yet), it auto-extracts and retries. Real loading/error/empty states with a retry button.
- `MatchDetail.jsx`: the fabricated fit-breakdown (score±6 etc.), confidence %, "Why now"/"Why not higher" copy and fake source links are gone — replaced by the engine's real `vectorScore`/`attributeScore` bars and its `reasons` as "Match signals".
- `AuthGate.jsx`: removed the fake "demo mode" login entirely; the session now stores the real `user.id` (needed by the matching engine), so **existing logins are invalidated — everyone must sign in again once**.
- `vite.config.js`: proxies `/api/matches` → matching engine `:3002`, `/api/extract` → extract agent `:3003`, everything else `/api` → gateway `:3000`.

**Backend/infra (to make the real APIs actually respond)**
- Created `.env` for the matching engine and extract agent pointing at the gateway's Postgres (their `.env.example`s referenced a non-existent `dealflow` DB).
- Installed pgvector into the running `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1` container and created the `extracted_profiles` schema. Note: that install is lost if the container is recreated — the durable fix is changing that project's compose image from `postgres:16` to `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (same data volume works).
- Started both services and restarted the preview server with a fresh build. Verified via curl: register → profile → extract → matches all flow correctly through the proxy.

## Things that don't exist in the backend (as you asked me to report)

1. **`OPENAI_API_KEY` is empty — this is the one hard blocker.** The extract agent gets a 401 from the FPT cloud API, so no embeddings → no matches can be generated yet. Put a key in `backend/agent/extract/.env` and restart that service; the whole flow then works with zero code changes. Until then the UI shows an honest error with retry.
2. **Customers, Partners & mentors, and Talent tabs have no backend API** — the matching engine only does founder↔investor. Those tabs now show "not connected to a backend service yet" instead of fake cards. Building those match types would be the next backend step.
3. **Source links / "public sources checked"** — no backend provides this; removed from the detail page rather than faking it.
4. **Outreach draft email** — there's no backend/LLM endpoint for this; it remains a client-side template (`lib/draft.js`) filled with your real profile + real candidate data. Tell me if you'd rather remove it or want an endpoint for it.
5. **Consent and draft/ready status** still have no backend columns — they stay in localStorage as before.
6. Port note: the extract agent runs on **3003** locally because Uptime Kuma occupies its default 3001.

Nothing is committed yet — say the word if you want me to commit/push.

## Summary
- Remove all frontend mock data (investors/partners/customers/talent datasets, demo-mode login, fabricated fit-breakdown/confidence/"why now" copy)
- Matches view now calls the matching engine for real: founders → `GET /matches/founders/:userId/investors`, investors → `GET /matches/investors/:userId/founders`, with automatic profile extraction (`POST /extract/profile`), loading/error/empty states, and real vector/attribute scores + match reasons in the detail view
- Vite proxy routes `/api/matches` → matching engine (:3002) and `/api/extract` → extract agent (:3003 locally; 3001 is taken by Uptime Kuma) alongside the gateway (:3000)
- Extract agent works without `OPENAI_API_KEY`: falls back to deterministic local feature-hash embeddings (lexical similarity); investor profiles now map to investor-shaped attributes and comma-joined sectors are split so attribute scoring works
- Profile form/API extended with contact + investor metric fields

## Notes
- Customers / Partners / Talent tabs have no backend service yet — they show an honest "not connected" notice
- Requires pgvector in Postgres (`extracted_profiles` table); tested end-to-end: founder + 2 investors, agri fund ranked #1 at 70/100 with real reasons, fintech fund 6/100
- Gateway needs a restart after deploy so `sequelize.sync({ alter: true })` adds the new profile columns

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
